spec_revision: 2 (iterated 2026-05-17 — HMAC provenance via pair code, expired-root path, TOCTOU capacity, metrics, remote arm)

tasks.title: Internal CA + HMAC-derive + capacity + regenerate-root + metrics (MESHSAT-595)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/001-pair-protocol/{requirements.md, design.md, data-model.md, contracts/openapi.yaml, contracts/schemas/pair-csr.json, contracts/schemas/pair-cert-bundle.json, contracts/schemas/pair-event.json} AND the relevant ADRs (adr/0002 CGO=0, adr/0005 bridge-issued certs, adr/0006 HMAC pair-code provenance). ALSO read internal/pair/state.go (MESHSAT-594, existing 316-line state machine) — your CA work integrates with it via Acquire/Release transitions; do NOT bypass. SECOND: verify the existing /api/pair/{arm,claim,status} endpoint skeleton (MESHSAT-596/598) honours REQ-002 (armed-state filter), REQ-003 (60s timeout transition), REQ-004 (re-arm refresh) — add tests if absent, fix wiring if broken; new logic goes in the existing handlers, not new files. THIRD: implement per the spec, covering REQ-009 through REQ-013 + REQ-020 + REQ-022 (HKDF derivation half — touch UI rendering half is T-002) + REQ-024 + REQ-025. New ca_root table via append-only migration v50. crypto/ecdsa + crypto/elliptic.P256() + golang.org/x/crypto/hkdf from stdlib (CGO_ENABLED=0 per Constitution Article II). Wrap CA private key via existing internal/keystore/ master-key facility (AES-256-GCM); explicit Zeroize on unwrapped key after each sign. HKDF derivation per ADR-0006: shared_secret = HKDF-SHA256(salt=bridge_id, ikm=pair_code, info='meshsat-pair-v1', length=32). Daily leaf-expiry check goroutine emits pair.ca.leaf-near-expiry audit events when within 7 days (REQ-012). First-boot generation of root is idempotent (skip if row exists, REQ-013). Expired-root path: refuse all claims with 503 ca-expired UNTIL operator POSTs /api/pair/regenerate-root with explicit confirm string (REQ-024). Capacity check: atomic SELECT COUNT + INSERT within single SQLite transaction to prevent TOCTOU (REQ-020). Prometheus counters per REQ-025 — wire into existing internal/api/ /metrics handler. Tests cover all REQ scenarios from spec/001-pair-protocol/acceptance/pair-protocol.feature that map to T-001's REQ-NNNs.

tasks.title: Touch-arm UI — Vue component + countdown + pair-code display + confirmation + rejection + remote-arm (MESHSAT-606)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/001-pair-protocol/{requirements.md, design.md, contracts/openapi.yaml} AND steering/coding-standards.md (Vue 3 Composition API + Tailwind only) + steering/repo-conventions.md (kiosk-mode constraints: touch-only, no hover states, large tap targets ≥44px). Cover REQ-001, REQ-016, REQ-017, REQ-018, REQ-022 (display the 6-char pair code prominently in monospace large font — operator must read aloud or type it), REQ-023 (remote-arm button in SPA — calls /api/pair/arm with operator-session-id). New Vue 3 Composition-API components in web/src/views/ + web/src/components/. PairArmingView is the parent (one new route entry in web/src/router.js — the ONLY line you may add there). Sub-components: PairCountdown (60→0 ring, 1s tick), PairCodeDisplay (renders pair_code from GET /api/pair/status in large monospace, NEVER copy-to-clipboard since that's a leak vector), PairConfirmation (5s post-success banner with client_label), PairRejection (3s rejection-reason indicator), PairRemoteArmButton (operator-only button; checks session auth before POST). API client in web/src/api/pairProtocol.js — extracted for unit-testability. Poll GET /api/pair/status every 1s while armed. Playwright e2e spec covers all 6 transitions (arm → code-display → countdown → confirmation OR rejection, plus remote-arm path). Use existing tactical design tokens; dark theme primary.
tasks.acceptance_test: ssh ansible@nllei01androidsdk01 'cd /home/claude-runner/gitlab/products/cubeos/meshsat && cd web && npm install && npx playwright test --grep "Pair"' || (cd /home/claude-runner/gitlab/products/cubeos/meshsat/web && npm install && npx playwright test --grep 'Pair')

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test — full pair handshake + boundary + capacity + metrics (MESHSAT-607)
tasks.prompt: FIRST: read spec/001-pair-protocol/{requirements.md, design.md, acceptance/pair-protocol.feature} AND the existing harness shape in test/integration/fullstack_e2e_test.go (use the same setup/teardown helpers). Cover REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-014, REQ-015, REQ-019, REQ-020, REQ-021, REQ-025. Five test files, each focused: (1) pair_e2e_test.go — happy path: arm, valid claim, cert+JWT mint, paired_client row, audit_log entry, Hub event queued; re-call protected endpoint with new cert+JWT, assert 200 (REQ-005, 014, 015). (2) pair_capacity_test.go — 10 paired_client rows pre-seeded + 11th claim → 429 + no new audit_log/paired_client (REQ-020). (3) pair_boundary_test.go — claim at second 59.5 with 500ms processing → completes successfully (REQ-021). (4) pair_metrics_test.go — invoke arm + completed-claim + failed-claim + verify /metrics counters incremented (REQ-025). (5) pair_perf_test.go — 100 sequential claims, assert p99 < 800ms (REQ-019). Three fixture files: valid CSR + invalid-sig variant + bad-hmac variant. Use the same test-mode flag the existing harness uses (in-memory SQLite, no Hub uplink). Tests should pass against fresh `go test -count=1 -timeout 300s -tags=integration ./test/integration/...`.
